food_subgroup: Fried chicken — fast food, bone-in, battered and deep-fried

ontology.foodon_gap_note: No FoodOn term exists specifically for Jollibee Chickenjoy. FOODON_03302050 (fried chicken food product) is the closest defensible parent class.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 code A0CKP (Chicken meat, fried) is the closest parent; no brand-specific Jollibee Chickenjoy code exists in the FoodEx2 catalogue.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: SNOMED CT 255620007 (Fried chicken) is the closest available concept; no brand-specific entry exists.

serving.natural_language_portion: One realistic serving is one piece of Jollibee Chickenjoy fried chicken, weighing approximately 130 g, representing one-sixth of the 6-piece bucket.

nutrient_source.title: Chicken, broilers or fryers, meat and skin, cooked, fried, batter (USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy, NDB 05062)
nutrient_source.organization: United States Department of Agriculture (USDA) Agricultural Research Service
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: USDA SR Legacy entry for batter-fried chicken (meat and skin, bone-in) is used as the closest defensible analogue for Jollibee Chickenjoy original. Jollibee does not publish full per-100 g nutrient panels for Singapore. Values are adapted from USDA NDB 05062 (chicken, broilers or fryers, meat and skin, fried, batter). Sodium is adjusted upward to reflect Jollibee's known seasoning profile based on published Jollibee Philippines nutrition disclosures (approximate 700–800 mg per piece), scaled to per-100 g basis.

gi_source.title: International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2021 — Atkinson FS et al., American Journal of Clinical Nutrition
gi_source.organization: American Journal of Clinical Nutrition / University of Sydney GI Database
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for Jollibee Chickenjoy. GI of 45 is estimated from batter-fried chicken analogues reported in the University of Sydney GI Database and Atkinson et al. 2021 international GI tables. Batter-fried chicken has a low-to-medium GI driven by the wheat flour batter; the protein and fat content substantially blunt postprandial glucose response. GL per serving calculated as (GI × carbohydrate_g per serving) / 100 = (45 × 12.7) / 100 ≈ 5.7, where carbohydrate per 130 g serving = 9.8 × 1.30 = 12.74 g.

hypertension.key_factor: High sodium content (~580 mg per 100 g; ~754 mg per piece)
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should limit Chickenjoy consumption to occasional servings, as a single piece provides approximately 33% of the WHO recommended daily sodium limit of 2000 mg, and a 6-piece bucket delivers approximately 4500 mg sodium — more than double the daily limit.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Moderate glycemic load per piece (GL ≈ 5.7) but high total fat and caloric density in bucket portions
type2_diabetes.guidance: People with Type 2 Diabetes may consume one piece of Chickenjoy occasionally within a carbohydrate-controlled meal plan, as the low GI (≈45) and moderate GL per piece are manageable, but the high saturated fat and caloric density of a full bucket (≈1600 kcal) pose risks for insulin resistance and weight management.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Saturated fat, trans fat, cholesterol, and high sodium in a deep-fried battered product
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Individuals with cardiovascular disease should treat Chickenjoy as an occasional food only, as the combination of saturated fat (~4.5 g/100 g), trans fat (~0.1 g/100 g), cholesterol (~88 mg/100 g), and high sodium (~580 mg/100 g) collectively elevates LDL cholesterol and blood pressure risk when consumed regularly or in large portions.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Jollibee Chickenjoy is the signature fried chicken product of Jollibee, a Filipino fast-food chain with a strong presence in Singapore, particularly at halal-certified outlets such as Jollibee Bugis Village. The 6-piece bucket (Original flavour) contains six bone-in, skin-on chicken pieces — typically a mix of drumsticks, thighs, and wings — that are marinated in a proprietary blend of spices, coated in a seasoned wheat flour batter, and deep-fried in vegetable oil until golden and crispy. The result is a juicy, tender interior with a distinctively crunchy, savoury crust that is slightly sweet compared to Western-style fried chicken, reflecting Filipino flavour preferences. Chickenjoy is widely regarded as a comfort food and a cultural icon in Filipino cuisine. In Singapore, it is popular among Filipino expatriates, halal-conscious consumers, and fried chicken enthusiasts. Nutritionally, each piece provides approximately 360 kcal, 29 g protein, 22 g fat, and 13 g carbohydrate. The product is high in sodium (~754 mg per piece) and saturated fat, making it a high-concern food for individuals managing hypertension or cardiovascular disease. The glycemic index is estimated at approximately 45 (low), driven by the batter, but the glycemic load per piece is moderate at ~5.7. The 6-piece bucket retails at approximately SGD 22.50 on field survey data. Clinically, this product should be treated as an occasional indulgence rather than a regular dietary staple, particularly for individuals with cardiometabolic conditions.
common_user_queries: How many calories in Jollibee Chickenjoy?
Can diabetics eat Jollibee fried chicken?

confidence_reason: Jollibee Singapore does not publish a full per-100 g nutrient panel for the Singapore market. Nutrient values are adapted from USDA SR Legacy batter-fried chicken (NDB 05062), which is the closest defensible analogue in an approved primary source. Sodium has been adjusted upward to reflect Jollibee's known seasoning intensity. GI is an analogous estimate from the University of Sydney GI Database and Atkinson et al. 2021. Confidence is therefore medium rather than high.
notes: 1. Vitamin D is -1 as it is not reported in the USDA SR Legacy source entry for batter-fried chicken (NDB 05062). 2. Nutrient values are per 100 g of edible portion (meat, skin, and batter coating combined), bone excluded. 3. The 6-piece bucket serving weight is approximately 780 g total (6 × 130 g per piece), yielding approximately 2160 kcal per bucket. 4. Halal certification applies to Jollibee Singapore outlets; the halal flag is set to true based on Muis-certified status of Jollibee Singapore. 5. Trans fat is reported as approximately 0.1 g/100 g based on USDA data for batter-fried chicken; this is a trace level from naturally occurring trans fats in poultry fat rather than industrial hydrogenation. 6. Vitamin C is 0.0 g/100 g; this is confirmed zero as fried chicken contains no meaningful vitamin C content per USDA FoodData Central. 7. GI is an analogous estimate; no direct GI measurement for Jollibee Chickenjoy was found in the University of Sydney GI Database or peer-reviewed literature as of the accessed date. 8. monounsaturated_fat_g, polyunsaturated_fat_g, trans_fat_g, potassium_mg, calcium_mg, iron_mg, cholesterol_mg, vitamin_a_mcg_rae, vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg, vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg, vitamin_b3_niacin_mg, vitamin_b6_mg, vitamin_b12_mcg, folate_mcg_dfe, vitamin_e_mg, phosphorus_mg, magnesium_mg, zinc_mg, selenium_mcg, and water_g are all sourced from USDA SR Legacy NDB 05062 and are present as numeric values rather than -1 where reported.

improvement_actions_taken: Constructed canonical English food name from input menu name.
Added all mandatory multilingual local names (Chinese simplified, Chinese traditional, Malay, Tamil, Hindi) per G6 and Q1 rules.
Sourced per-100g nutrient values from USDA SR Legacy NDB 05062 (batter-fried chicken), the closest approved analogue.
Adjusted sodium upward from USDA baseline to reflect Jollibee seasoning profile.
Estimated GI at 45 (low) from University of Sydney GI Database analogues and Atkinson et al. 2021.
Documented vitamin_d_mcg as -1 with explicit note per G3 sentinel value rule.
Documented vitamin_c_mg as 0.0 with confirmed zero justification in notes.
Set all three health context conditions with specific clinical guidance.
Verified aliases_by_language and flat aliases are perfectly consistent.
Ensured all ontology match_type values use only allowed strings (exact, closest_parent, none).
Wrote natural_language_description exceeding 200 characters with full clinical and cultural context.
